Report Section / Category: Analysis of 2,018 subjects across 14 body measurements

Additional Information: Scale together more than any other pair
Similar heights can have vastly different torso sizes

Values / Interpretation: Second highest reflects body fat distribution
Baseline measurements for comparison (all in cm)
Clear pattern from fixed to modifiable characteristics
Can use interchangeably for screening (needs validation)
Most modifiable aspect of body composition
Cannot estimate torso from height - must measure directly

Details / Measurements: Skeletal measurements show tight proportions and high consistency, while soft tissue measurements display greater variability
Body composition and muscular development create the most variation - bicep and waist show 3x more variability than skeletal
Multi-dimensional measurement protocols are essential - single measurements or height-based estimates mask important variation
For health and fitness applications, focus on soft tissue measurements (waist, chest, limb circumferences) as these reflect
modifiable characteristics and show the greatest inter-individual variation
This analysis provides baseline correlation values and variability ranges for future studies, population health assessments,
